name: Initiation
summary: Dashboard requirements extracted. 14 user stories defined. Low complexity (15/50). Internal project — no budget constraints. Condition: confirm demo audience expectations with Stan.

documents.title: Context Analysis
documents.subtitle: Internal tool, low risk profile
documents.content:
The Combo D Dashboard is an internal tool for visualizing PM Agent Chain outputs. As a client-facing viewer, it has minimal compliance requirements and no external stakeholder complexity.

The project brief is well-defined: 3 screens in Sprint 1, enhanced panels in Sprint 2, and polish in Sprint 3. The development approach (Next.js, Tailwind, JSON data) is pre-selected and appropriate for the scope.

documents.title: Project Charter
documents.subtitle: Internal, self-sponsored
documents.content:
The project is self-sponsored with no formal budget ceiling. Success criteria: functional dashboard deployed to Vercel, capable of displaying PM Agent Chain results for any project.

Timeline: 3 sprints over 1 week. No external dependencies. Single developer with full authority to make technical decisions.

documents.title: Statement of Work
documents.subtitle: 8 deliverables across 3 sprints
documents.content: Sprint 1: Project selector, project overview, context setup. Sprint 2: Document viewer, KB explorer, governance tracker. Sprint 3: Assessment viewer, pipeline polish, multi-project support, deployment.

documents.title: Business Case
documents.subtitle: Internal efficiency tool
documents.content: The dashboard eliminates the need for PMO leaders to access Claude Code directly. It provides a read-only, client-safe view of chain results that can be shared in executive briefings.

documents.title: Requirements Catalog
documents.subtitle: 14 user stories, all Must-Have
documents.content: All 14 requirements are Must-Have for the MVP. No Could-Have or Won't-Have items — the scope is intentionally minimal and well-bounded.

documents.title: Stakeholder Register
documents.subtitle: 2 stakeholders, aligned
documents.content: Primary stakeholder: Stan (PMO lead, demo audience). Secondary: development team (internal). Full alignment on scope and approach.

documents.title: Co-Pilot Analysis
documents.subtitle: 1 bias detected, 2 blind spots
documents.content: Low-complexity internal project with clear scope. One bias detected: estimation optimism — sprint scope ambitious for single developer. Blind spots: no iPad physical testing plan, no demo script for stakeholder walkthrough.

documents.title: Knowledge Log
documents.subtitle: 12 entries, 4 clusters
documents.content: 12 knowledge entries captured during initiation. Primary clusters: Technical Architecture (5), UI/UX Patterns (3), Data Structure (2), Deployment (2). All entries are informational — no critical findings.

name: Planning
summary: 3-sprint WBS with 22 tasks. Bottom-up estimate: 18 dev hours. Schedule realistic for single developer. Condition: budget for iPad testing device if not available.

documents.title: Context Analysis
documents.subtitle: Sprint planning assumptions verified
documents.content: Verified assumptions from Initiation: Next.js scaffolding takes ~5 min, Tailwind v4 uses CSS-based config (no tailwind.config.js), Vercel CLI available for deploy. All assumptions held.

documents.title: WBS
documents.subtitle: 22 tasks across 3 sprints
documents.content: Sprint 1 (8 tasks): scaffold, design system, data layer, sidebar, project selector, project overview, context setup, deploy. Sprint 2 (8 tasks): document viewer, KB explorer, governance tracker, data enrichment, mobile pass, testing. Sprint 3 (6 tasks): assessment viewer, radar chart, pipeline polish, multi-project, final polish, production deploy.

documents.title: Project Schedule
documents.subtitle: 3 sprints, ~18 dev hours total
documents.content: Sprint 1: 6 hours. Sprint 2: 7 hours. Sprint 3: 5 hours. Total: ~18 hours across 2-3 days. Critical path: data structure decisions in Sprint 1 gate all subsequent work.

documents.title: Budget
documents.subtitle: $0 — internal project, no costs
documents.content: Zero external costs. Vercel free tier sufficient. No vendor licensing. Developer time is the only resource. Claude Code usage covered by existing subscription.

documents.title: Risk Register
documents.subtitle: 5 risks, all low-medium
documents.content: R1: Scope creep from feature ideas (MEDIUM). R2: Tailwind v4 breaking changes (LOW). R3: Mobile responsive gaps discovered late (MEDIUM). R4: Data structure doesn't scale to real chain output (LOW). R5: Demo audience expectations misaligned (LOW).

documents.title: Sprint Plan
documents.subtitle: 3 sprints with clear deliverables
documents.content: Each sprint has entry criteria (previous sprint deployed), exit criteria (build passes, deployed to Vercel), and a demo checkpoint. Sprint boundaries are hard — no feature carryover.

documents.title: Communication Plan
documents.subtitle: Git commits as progress reports
documents.content: Progress tracked via git commits. Each sprint ends with a deployment and summary. Stan receives the Vercel URL after Sprint 3 for review.

documents.title: Estimation Paradigm Note
documents.subtitle: AI-assisted dev changes estimation rules
documents.content: Traditional estimation models don't apply to AI-assisted development. Tasks that would take 4-8 hours manually complete in 20-40 minutes with Claude Code. The WBS reflects AI-assisted estimates, not traditional ones. This creates a new pattern: estimation-paradigm-mismatch.

documents.title: Co-Pilot Analysis
documents.subtitle: Estimation paradigm flagged
documents.content: The Co-Pilot flags that WBS estimates assume consistent AI-assisted velocity. If the AI tool hits context limits or generates errors, tasks could take 3-5x longer. No contingency for AI-tool failure mode.

documents.title: Knowledge Log
documents.subtitle: 6 new entries
documents.content: 6 new entries bring total to 18. New dominant cluster: Estimation Paradigm (3 entries). Key insight: AI-assisted development creates a bimodal velocity distribution — either very fast or debugging-slow, with little middle ground.

name: Execution
summary: All 3 sprints executed successfully. 22/22 tasks complete. Zero blockers. Velocity exceeded estimates by 40%. Dogfooding revealed 2 UX improvements.

documents.title: Sprint 1 Report
documents.subtitle: Complete — 6 hours, all tasks done
documents.content: Sprint 1 delivered: project scaffold, design system, data layer, sidebar, project selector page, project overview page, context setup page, initial Vercel deployment. All exit criteria met.

documents.title: Sprint 2 Report
documents.subtitle: Complete — 5.5 hours, all tasks done
documents.content: Sprint 2 delivered: enhanced document viewer with content, KB explorer with sortable table and prevention playbook, governance tracker with inline editing. Came in under estimate (5.5 vs 7 hours).

documents.title: Sprint 3 Report
documents.subtitle: Complete — 4 hours, all tasks done
documents.content: Sprint 3 delivered: SVG radar chart, assessment viewer, pipeline with connectors, multi-project support, breadcrumbs, meta tags, favicon, mobile responsive pass. Under estimate (4 vs 5 hours).

documents.title: Velocity Analysis
documents.subtitle: 40% faster than estimated
documents.content: Actual total: ~15.5 hours vs 18 hours estimated. AI-assisted development velocity was consistent across sprints. No significant debugging delays. The estimation-paradigm-mismatch pattern confirmed: AI estimates should use 0.7x multiplier for well-scoped tasks.

documents.title: Dogfooding Findings
documents.subtitle: 2 UX issues discovered
documents.content: Issue 1: Mobile hamburger menu overlaps page title at 375px — needs left padding on main content. Issue 2: Combo D project card still shows stale data from initial scaffold — needs update to reflect actual completed sprints.

documents.title: Change Requests
documents.subtitle: 0 CRs — scope held
documents.content: Zero change requests during execution. Scope discipline maintained across all 3 sprints. Feature ideas (auth, database, real-time updates) captured in backlog but not allowed to interrupt sprints.

documents.title: Blocker Register
documents.subtitle: 0 blockers
documents.content: No execution blockers encountered. The closest issue was a TypeScript type mismatch between Agent data (nullable runTime) and component props, resolved in <2 minutes.

documents.title: Resource Utilization
documents.subtitle: Single developer, AI-augmented
documents.content: One developer with Claude Code. No additional resources needed. AI tool handled scaffolding, component generation, data file creation, and deployment. Developer focused on architecture decisions and quality review.

documents.title: Co-Pilot Analysis
documents.subtitle: Clean execution, no concerns
documents.content: Execution was clean. The Co-Pilot notes that the project benefited from clear scope, single decision-maker, and no external dependencies — conditions that are atypical for enterprise projects.

documents.title: Knowledge Log
documents.subtitle: 5 new entries
documents.content: 5 new entries bring total to 23. Key new patterns: ai-velocity-multiplier (AI dev is ~1.4x faster than estimated), scope-discipline-holds (zero CRs when sprints are short and well-bounded), dogfooding-reveals-gaps (using your own product finds issues testing doesn't).

name: Monitoring
summary: 7/8 gate criteria PASS. Go-live confidence: 85%. 3 conditions for full approval. Primary gap: no physical iPad test yet.

documents.title: KPI Dashboard
documents.subtitle: 7/8 criteria passing
documents.content:
Passing: Scope Complete (YES), Schedule Met (YES), Budget Met (YES — $0), Quality Gate (YES — build passes), Deployment Live (YES), Mobile Responsive (YES — tested at 375px/768px/1440px), Multi-Project (YES).

At risk: Physical Device Test (NOT YET — tested via browser DevTools only).

documents.title: Gate Assessment
documents.subtitle: CONDITIONAL GO recommendation
documents.content: The project meets all criteria for a conditional go-live. Three conditions must be met before presenting to enterprise prospects: (1) Stan Bryant reviews and approves the dashboard, (2) a demo script is prepared, (3) physical iPad test confirms the mobile experience matches browser simulation.

documents.title: Risk Status
documents.subtitle: 4/5 risks closed
documents.content: R1 Scope Creep: CLOSED — zero CRs. R2 Tailwind v4: CLOSED — no breaking changes. R3 Mobile Gaps: MITIGATED — responsive pass done, physical test pending. R4 Data Scale: CLOSED — JSON structure works for demo. R5 Demo Expectations: OPEN — awaiting Stan's review.

documents.title: Earned Value Report
documents.subtitle: Under budget, ahead of schedule
documents.content: Planned Value: 18 hours. Earned Value: 18 hours (100% scope complete). Actual Cost: 15.5 hours. CPI: 1.16 (under budget). SPI: 1.0 (on schedule). The project delivered full scope 14% under estimated effort.

documents.title: Governance Report
documents.subtitle: 3 conditional items
documents.content: Three governance items tracked: (1) Stan Bryant dashboard preview — deadline April 11. (2) Demo script for enterprise prospects — no hard deadline but needed before first demo. (3) Physical iPad test — can be done anytime with device access.

documents.title: Deployment Status
documents.subtitle: Live on Vercel, 3 production deploys
documents.content: Dashboard live at combo-d-dashboard.vercel.app. Three production deployments (one per sprint). Build time: ~12 seconds. Zero deployment failures. GitHub repo connected for push-to-deploy.

documents.title: Quality Metrics
documents.subtitle: Zero TypeScript errors, clean build
documents.content: TypeScript strict mode: zero errors. ESLint: clean. Build: passes consistently. No runtime errors in production. Lighthouse performance score: estimated 90+ (static pages with minimal JS).

documents.title: Co-Pilot Analysis
documents.subtitle: Ready for stakeholder review
documents.content: The dashboard is functionally complete and demo-ready. The Co-Pilot recommends scheduling Stan's review this week while momentum is high. The conditional items are low-effort and can be resolved in a single session.

documents.title: Knowledge Log
documents.subtitle: 6 new entries
documents.content: 6 new entries bring total to 29. New patterns: conditional-go-is-the-norm (most internal tools ship with conditions), earned-value-works-for-small-projects (EV tracking validated even at micro scale), deploy-early-deploy-often (3 deploys in 2 days kept feedback loop tight).

name: Validation
summary: Awaiting monitoring completion to begin validation phase.
